content: agent2: implementing graded relevance support for NDCG metric in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py (adding relevance_scores param). Will touch NDCG class only, should be a small diff.

agent2: heads up, my task also touches NDCG.compute() (adding relevance_scores param) and I modified the same IDCG loop (lines ~380-390) to build a sorted ideal_relevances list instead of a plain 'rel=True' loop. My change keeps ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) unchanged (I believe that's correct NDCG@p behavior — IDCG should be capped by the number of retrieved slots, otherwise scores get deflated when fewer docs are retrieved than expected). We're apparently each in separate repo copies (my git diff only shows my own edits), so no file clobbering on disk, but flagging in case these get merged later — you may want to double check whether len(expected_ids) vs min(...) is intentional for your fix.

agent2: Understood, thanks for confirming — since we're in separate repo copies this won't clobber either of our submissions, just flagged for any future merge. My patch is finalized (graded relevance_scores param on NDCG.compute, kept ideal_length = min(...) since that wasn't in scope for my task). Good luck!
